checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 income statement and cash flow statement

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=60643587.49 (2013-10-01 to 2013-12-31) - single quarter figure driving the whole year positive result despite negative OCF each quarter

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet=29597324.11 (2013 annual) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=72893821.08 (2014 annual) - inventory grew ~466% while revenue grew ~146%

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=38989859.83 (2015-03-31) growing to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=46996771.27 (2015-06-30) while allowance fell

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual and quarterly filings from 10-K filed 2012-03-14 (covering FY2010) through 10-Q filed 2015-08-10 (covering Q2 2015), across Assets, Revenues, AR, Inventory, Allowance, Liabilities, Cash Flow and Equity concepts

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Premature or aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., bill-and-hold, channel stuffing, or recognizing revenue ahead of collectability) combined with an inadequate/declining bad-debt allowance, inflating both revenue and net receivables while understating credit-loss reserves.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=46996771.27 (2015-06-30) vs quarterly SalesRevenueGoodsNet=28842414.58 (2015 Q2) implies DSO near 150 days, up sharply from DSO implied by AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=9082048.73 (2013-12-31) vs annual SalesRevenueGoodsNet=29597324.11 (2013)

AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1582874.83 (2015-03-31) dropped to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=317792.56 (2015-06-30) even as receivables grew from 38989859.83 to 46996771.27 over the same period

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Failure to write down obsolete/slow-moving inventory or improper capitalization of costs into inventory, causing inventory to balloon far faster than sales and understating cost of goods sold/expensed costs.

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet=2348742.73 (2013-12-31) grew to InventoryNet=13301018.95 (2014-12-31), a ~466% increase, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only ~146% (from 29597324.11 in 2013 to 72893821.08 in 2014)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A large non-operating or non-cash gain (e.g., derivative/warrant revaluation, bargain purchase, or debt extinguishment gain) recorded in Q4 2013 appears to have driven full-year net income far above operating results and cash generation, masking weak underlying operating cash performance.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=60643587.49 (2013-10-01 to 2013-12-31) versus OperatingIncomeLoss=1803259.71 (same quarter) and full-year OperatingIncomeLoss=-3449449.53 (2013 annual) versus full-year NetIncomeLossAvailableToCommonStockholdersBasic=50698263.18 (2013 annual)
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-16156281.62 (2013 annual) remained deeply negative despite the large reported net income

top_signals: Accounts receivable ballooned far faster than revenue, with implied DSO reaching ~150 days by Q2 2015 (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=46996771.27 vs quarterly SalesRevenueGoodsNet=28842414.58)
Allowance for doubtful accounts collapsed from 1,582,874.83 (2015-03-31) to 317,792.56 (2015-06-30) even as receivables continued growing, suggesting under-reserving
Inventory grew ~466% (2013 to 2014) while revenue grew only ~146%, indicating potential inventory overstatement or obsolescence risk

Persistent, large divergence between net income and operating cash flow across multiple years (e.g., NI=50,698,263.18 vs OCF=-16,156,281.62 in 2013), including an unexplained $60.6M net income quarter (Q4 2013) far exceeding operating income
Two consecutive years of NT 10-K late-filing notices (2014-03-17 and 2015-03-17) indicating difficulty closing the books on time
